signals.name: active_connections
signals.description: number of active database connections used by workers and reconcilers

signals.name: idle_in_transaction
signals.description: connections holding transactions open without progress

signals.name: claim_latency_ms
signals.description: time spent acquiring a job lease

signals.name: commit_latency_ms
signals.description: time spent validating fencing token and writing commit log

example_queries.pool_state: SELECT state, count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;
example_queries.idle_in_transaction: SELECT pid, now() - xact_start AS duration, query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'idle in transaction';
example_queries.worker_connections: SELECT application_name, count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY application_name;

operator_actions: increase pool size only if database CPU and lock waits are healthy
reduce worker concurrency when lock waits dominate
use batch claiming to reduce round trips
increase lease duration when workers consistently exceed lease window
